name: Chestnut Groves and Castle of Constantina Trail
name_original: Castillo de Constantina y Monasterio Nuestra Señora de los Ángeles - desde Constantina — circular por Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla
description: Hike a moderate 5.1-mile (8.3 km) circular route from Constantina, exploring its castle, chestnut forest, and the Rivera del Huéznar.
region_intro: Hiking around Constantina offers access to diverse natural landscapes within the Sierra Morena Natural Park in Seville, Andalusia. The region is characterized by lush hills, valleys, and significant geological formations such as the Cerro del Hierro Natural Monument. Trails often follow the scenic Rivera del Huéznar, passing through chestnut forests and providing varied terrain for outdoor activities. This area presents a range of hiking opportunities, from gentle strolls to more challenging ascents. Best hiking trails around Constantina The most popular hiking route is Chestnut Groves and Castle of Constantina Trail, a 5.1 miles (8.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 15 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the town's castle, chestnut forest, and the Rivera del Huéznar. Another top favourite among local hikers is Via Verde de la Sierra - from Estacion de Cazalla — circular through the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park, a moderate 8.3 miles (13.4 km) path. This circular route traverses the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park, offering varied natural scenery. Local hikers also love the Los Castañares Trail loop from Constantina, a 3.5 miles (5.7 km) trail leading through chestnut forests, often completed in about 1 hour 34 minutes. Hiking around Constantina is defined by the rolling hills of the Sierra Morena Natural Park, scenic river valleys like the Rivera del Huéznar, and ancient chestnut forests. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Constantina are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 800 hikers have used komoot to explore Constantina's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Constantina?
A: Constantina offers a selection of &lt;em&gt;over 15 hiking trails&lt;/em&gt;, catering to various fitness levels. These routes explore the diverse landscapes of the Sierra Morena Natural Park, from gentle strolls through chestnut forests to more challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Constantina?
A: The terrain around Constantina is characterized by the rolling hills of the Sierra Morena Natural Park, scenic river valleys like the Rivera del Huéznar, and ancient chestnut forests. You'll find a mix of well-trodden paths, forest tracks, and some ascents, offering varied natural scenery.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Constantina?
A: Yes, Constantina has several easy and family-friendly options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31397115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Castañares Trail loop from Constantina&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 5.7 km (3.5 miles) route through chestnut forests, perfect for a leisurely walk. About half of the trails in the area are rated as easy.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Hiking around Constantina allows you to discover several notable natural features and landmarks. You can explore the unique geological formations of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4781135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Hierro Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, enjoy the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/736496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéznar River Landscape&lt;/a&gt;, and even visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4764750" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Huéznar Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;. Many trails also pass through ancient chestnut groves and offer views of the historic Castle of Constantina.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Constantina?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Constantina are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010107004/chestnut-groves-and-castle-of-constantina-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chestnut Groves and Castle of Constantina Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is an 8.3 km (5.1 miles) loop exploring the town's castle, forest, and the Rivera del Huéznar.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Constantina?
A: The best time to hike in Constantina is generally during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant for outdoor activities, and the natural landscapes are particularly vibrant with spring blooms or autumn colors. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Constantina?
A: While specific regulations can vary within the Sierra Morena Natural Park, generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in Constantina, especially if kept on a leash. It's always recommended to check local signage or park guidelines for any specific restrictions on certain routes or protected areas.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Constantina?
A: The hiking routes in Constantina are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 150 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse natural landscapes, the tranquility of the chestnut forests, and the well-maintained paths that offer a mix of easy and moderate challenges. The blend of natural beauty and historical sights like the castle is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any long-distance or more challenging hikes available?
A: For those seeking a longer and more challenging experience, Constantina offers options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10318804" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molino del Corcho Trail loop from Cazalla - Constantina&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult 20.2 km (12.6 miles) route provides a significant elevation gain and a rewarding journey through the natural park.

Q: Is there public transport or parking available near the trailheads?
A: Constantina is accessible by road, and parking is generally available within the town, often near popular starting points for trails. For public transport, bus services connect Constantina with Seville and other nearby towns. Some trails, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010114176/via-verde-de-la-sierra-from-estacion-de-cazalla-circular-through-the-sierra-norte-de-sevilla-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Verde de la Sierra - from Estacion de Cazalla&lt;/a&gt;, are accessible from specific train stations or villages within the natural park.

Q: Are there any places to eat or drink along the hiking routes?
A: While many trails venture into natural areas, Constantina itself offers various cafes, restaurants, and bars where you can refuel before or after your hike. For routes that pass through or near other villages within the Sierra Morena Natural Park, you might find local establishments. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on longer routes.
tips: The Moorish-era castle was declared a site of cultural interest in 1985. It can be easily reached on foot and by car. The panoramic views of the town are impressive.

Chestnut trees are trees that need humidity and altitude to grow properly. They can reach 30 meters in height and their wood is used for many purposes. This forest is especially spectacular in the fall, when the leaves turn gold and brown; in spring you can see the greenery of the new flowers from which the chestnuts will later be born.

This monastery, formerly located in the center of Constantina, was moved to the La Carlina estate, the point where it is today. In the 1950s, this property belonged to Léon Degrelle, a Belgian politician who collaborated with the Nazi regime during World War II and fled to the south of Spain to avoid punishment. In 1952 the construction of this Arab-style palace house began, to which annex buildings were later added.

Currently, the Hieronymite nuns who live in the monastery offer hospitality services. You can also visit the craft store where they sell sweets, soaps, liquors, etc.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: This circular hike usually takes around 2 hours and 15 minutes to complete. This estimate is for continuous walking and does not include stops for breaks or sightseeing.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is rated as moderate. While some paths in the wider area are easier, this specific route requires a good level of fitness due to its length and elevation changes.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail begins from the outskirts of Constantina, near the historic Chapel of La Hiedra. Parking is generally available in Constantina, especially near the town center or residential areas, though specific trailhead parking might require a short walk.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the historic Castle of Constantina, offering panoramic views of the town. You'll also traverse a beautiful chestnut forest, especially vibrant in autumn, and pass by the ecologically rich Rivera del Huéznar. The trail also includes the Start of the Castañares Trail and passes near the Monastery of Our Lady of the Angels.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a varied landscape within the Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla, including evergreen oak woodlands, meadows, and rolling hills. The path includes forest trails, small olive groves, and a notable chestnut forest. You will also cross railway tracks three times, two under bridges and one at a level crossing.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is particularly beautiful in autumn when the chestnut forest displays stunning reds and golds. Spring also offers pleasant temperatures and blooming flora. During warmer months, the chestnut forest provides welcome shade.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through areas with livestock (like Iberian pigs) or near sensitive wildlife habitats within the Natural Park.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to hike in Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla?
faqs.answer: Generally, no specific permits or entrance fees are required for hiking on established trails within the Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla. However, always respect local regulations and private property signs.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route intersects with sections of other well-known paths. Approximately 17% of the route overlaps with the Sendero de Sierra Morena, and another 17% with the [CIMA SV01] El Robledo - Cerro Negrillo trail.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink near the trailhead in Constantina?
faqs.answer: Yes, Constantina offers various options for refreshments. As a traditional white village, you'll find local restaurants, cafes, and bars in the town center where you can enjoy a meal or a drink before or after your hike.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter along the way?
faqs.answer: While hiking, you might spot Iberian pigs in livestock farms. The wider Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla is home to diverse fauna, including species like the Iberian Lynx, Black Stork, and Imperial Eagle, though sightings of these rarer animals are less common on the trail itself.

waypoints.name: Monastery of Our Lady of the Angels
